title: asterolasia buxifolia
intro: Asterolasia buxifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the mock box myrtle. It is native to Australia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, offering health benefits such as relief from pain and inflammation. Asterolasia buxifolia is prepared into herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
